🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European B2B SaaS startup adjacent to insurtech, building digital underwriting or embedded insurance distribution software for regulated brokers.”
2. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European seed-stage regulated-intermediary fintech startup similar to Zelros or Seyna automating compliance or policy management for insurance or wealth firms.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (14-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 14 total):
ai_workflow_automation weight=3 — 7 entries
in 1d — Wed May 20“Here is a European seed-stage AI workflow automation startup replacing manual document approval or data-entry loops for operations and finance teams in SMEs.”
in 2d — Thu May 21“Here is a European AI-native SaaS startup selling to Head of Legal or General Counsel, automating contract review, compliance checks, or document extraction.”
in 3d — Fri May 22“Here is a French AI workflow automation startup building LLM-powered tools for HR or finance teams in French SMEs and mid-market companies.”
in 4d — Sat May 23“Here is a pre-revenue European AI workflow co-pilot startup integrating with ERP or CRM to automate data entry and approval chains at pre-seed stage.”
in 5d — Sun May 24“Here is a European B2B SaaS startup building AI agents on top of n8n, Make, or Zapier APIs to automate multi-step enterprise workflows.”
in 6d — Mon May 25“Here is a European AI startup adjacent to robotic process automation, applying LLMs to invoice matching, purchase order approval, or expense categorization.”
in 7d — Tue May 26“Here is a European seed-stage AI-native workflow automation startup similar to Dust or Bardeen, targeting knowledge worker productivity in B2B context.”
regulated_intermediary_fintech weight=3 — 7 entries
in 8d — Wed May 27“Here is a European seed-stage B2B SaaS startup automating KYC, AML, or regulatory reporting for insurance brokers or wealth managers under MiFID II or AIFMD.”
in 9d — Thu May 28“Here is a European fintech startup selling to Chief Compliance Officers or Risk Managers at mid-market insurance or asset management firms.”
in 10d — Fri May 29“Here is a French insurtech or wealth-tech SaaS startup automating policy distribution or regulatory filing for ORIAS-registered intermediaries in France.”
in 11d — Sat May 30“Here is a pre-revenue European regulated-intermediary SaaS startup building DORA or IDD compliance tooling for insurance distribution at pre-seed stage.”
in 12d — Sun May 31“Here is a European fintech startup integrating with Open Banking or PSD2 APIs to automate payment reconciliation or AML screening for regulated payment providers.”
in 13d — Mon Jun 1“Here is a European B2B SaaS startup adjacent to insurtech, building digital underwriting or embedded insurance distribution software for regulated brokers.”

CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURL
bsurance90MATCHSTEP A, the company is a B2B SaaS platform for insurers, MGAs, and retail channels. STEP B, the company provides B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries (insurers, MGAs) in the financial sector, aligning with the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle.https://bsurance.com
SANDIS | Insurance Software90MATCHThe company's core business is B2B SaaS for insurance distribution, which aligns with 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' as it targets regulated intermediaries (MGAs, coverholders, insurance agencies) for policy distribution. It serves a multinational market including the EU.https://sandis.io
42 Underwriting Services AG10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails because 42 Underwriting Services AG is described as a Managing General Underwriter (MGU), which is a financial services intermediary, not a software product. It provides underwriting and scheme management services, which are core business functions within insurance, not a B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries automating workflows, nor an AI-native workflow automation product. The company's business model is explicitly an MGU and thus falls outside the software product scope.https://42uw.com
Reloa20NO MATCHThe company operates in the UK market for pension advisers, not France or the French-speaking European market, as required by the ai_workflow_automation angle. While it matches the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle's function, it does not meet the specified geographical requirement for France or EU-wide RegTech, as it is hyper-focused on UK Letters of Authority.https://reloa.ai
Parsewise90MATCHThe company, Parsewise, is a B2B SaaS decision-platform that converts unstructured data into insights for risk assessment, aligning with professional/institutional buyers (Step A). It specifically fits the `regulated_intermediary_fintech` angle by providing solutions for Insurance & Reinsurance, Asset Management, and Regulatory bodies, which are regulated intermediaries, automating document processing for diligence and compliance (Step B).https://parsewise.ai
Cluda30NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed for the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle because the company does not have a clear presence, traction, or stated go-to-market focus in France or the French-speaking European market. While Cluda targets regulated intermediaries (insurance brokers), which could align with the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle, the company's description does not mention any French-specific activity or focus. The AI-native workflow automation is not for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams generally, but specifically for insurance brokers.https://cluda.ai
AARON GROUP10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company provides flight retailing solutions for the travel industry (airlines, OTAs, TMCs), which does not align with 'AI-native workflow automation' for operations/finance/legal/HR or 'B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries' in fintech.https://aarongroup.net
Travel Data + Analytics GmbH10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because the company provides market data and analytics for the travel industry, which is not an early-stage B2B software product in AI workflow or regulated-intermediary technology, nor is it targeting operations, finance, legal, or HR teams with AI workflow automation.https://traveldataanalytics.de
Bratsi10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Bratsi is a services company providing SAP Concur implementation and consulting, not a B2B software product (SaaS, AI workflow, or regulated-intermediary technology).https://brat-si.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Kili Technology (anchor)
2. Nabla (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 5 companies → evaluated: 5 for Profund VC

Dust AI90MATCHSTEP 1: The company provides an AI operating system for enterprises, which is an AI-native workflow automation product sold to professional buyers (B2B SaaS). It explicitly states it is building an 'AI operating system for the enterprise' and 'universal AI primitives that make enterprise workflows more intelligent,' which aligns with the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle. While the company's country is not provided, the URL `dust.tt` contains a `.tt` ccTLD, but the company explicitly states its founders have French names and are based in France. The company was also cofounded by French entrepreneurs (Stanislas Polu, Gabriel Hubert, Henry Fontanier). Therefore, it aligns with 'ai_workflow_automation' with French founders, and it is a match.https://dust.tt/home/aboutnews.google.com/rss/articles/CBMikAFBVV95cUxQ
Calibre0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because Calibre is a consumer-facing e-book management software, not a B2B software product.https://calibre-ebook.com/news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMioAFBVV95cUxQ
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.

LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

Also, it is not focused on France or the French-speaking EU market.https://startupper.gr/news/123119/poudouleveis-gr-
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.

↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).

9fin40NO MATCHThe company's core business is an AI-native platform for global debt markets (B2B SaaS). However, it does not demonstrate a clear presence, traction, or stated go-to-market focus in France or the French-speaking European market required by the `ai_workflow_automation` angle. While it mentions coverage across North America, Europe, Latin America and Asia-Pacific, it does not specify France. It also does not align with the `regulated_intermediary_fintech` angle, as its focus is on debt markets rather than automating regulatory reporting, KYC/AML, client onboarding, or policy distribution for intermediaries like insurance brokers, wealth managers, or payment service providers under EU frameworks.https://www.scalablepod.it/en/startup/9fin/
Upvest40NO MATCHUpvest provides investment APIs for fintechs, falling under the regulated_intermediary_fintech angle. However, the provided description does not indicate a specific focus on France or French-regulated entities, which is a key criterion for this gate. Therefore, it's a NO MATCH for the regulated_intermediary_fintech angle. It is not an AI-native workflow automation product, so it does not match that angle.https://partechpartners.com/companies/upvest

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

sforbt
5 MATCH · 75 NO MATCH · 6% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. travel_erp_integrations“Here is a European startup adjacent to travel ERP integration providing NDC content aggregation middleware, travel data warehousing, or booking analytics infrastructure for enterprises.”
2. travel_erp_integrations“Here is a European travel back-office integration platform similar to SIAP or Datalex, selling ERP connectors and data middleware to TMCs and corporate travel departments.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 1d — Wed May 20“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 2d — Thu May 21“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
in 3d — Fri May 22“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
in 4d — Sat May 23“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
in 5d — Sun May 24“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
in 6d — Mon May 25“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
in 7d — Tue May 26“Here is a European corporate hotel booking platform similar to HRS Group or Roomex, targeting mid-market and enterprise buyers needing multi-property hotel consolidation.”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 7 entries
in 8d — Wed May 27“Here is a European SaaS platform automating TMC mid-office workflow including ticketing, queue management, and booking servicing for travel management companies.”
in 9d — Thu May 28“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
in 10d — Fri May 29“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
in 11d — Sat May 30“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”
in 12d — Sun May 31“Here is a European startup using robotic process automation or AI to handle GDS scripting, PNR management, and exception handling for travel management companies.”
in 13d — Mon Jun 1“Here is a European startup adjacent to TMC workflow in corporate meetings management, group travel operations, or event travel logistics automation for enterprises.”
in 14d — Tue Jun 2“Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS, targeting travel management companies needing agent productivity and booking servicing software.”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 7 entries
in 15d — Wed Jun 3“Here is a European B2B SaaS or fintech platform automating corporate hotel invoice capture and cross-border VAT recovery for finance directors managing large travel budgets.”
in 16d — Thu Jun 4“Here is a virtual card issuance and lodging payment reconciliation tool sold to CFOs and treasury teams in European multinationals to eliminate manual hotel invoice processing.”
in 17d — Fri Jun 5“Here is a French or German startup providing AI-powered hotel folio extraction and EU VAT reclaim automation for corporate travel finance teams.”
in 18d — Sat Jun 6“Here is a Here is an early-stage European fintech company at Seed or Series A automating PCI-compliant hotel payment workflows integrated with SAP or Oracle ERP systems.”
in 19d — Sun Jun 7“Here is a European startup using machine learning for multi-currency hotel invoice reconciliation and automatic GL coding into enterprise accounting systems for travel spend.”
in 20d — Mon Jun 8“Here is a European company adjacent to travel payments in corporate card management, spend analytics for lodging, or procurement payment orchestration for business travel programs.”
in 21d — Tue Jun 9“Here is a European corporate travel payment platform similar to AirPlus or Conferma Pay, targeting finance directors needing automated hotel payment and VAT recovery.”
travel_erp_integrations weight=3 — 7 entries
in 22d — Wed Jun 10“Here is a European B2B SaaS company building ERP integration middleware connecting corporate travel booking systems to SAP or Oracle for automated GL coding and invoice posting.”
in 23d — Thu Jun 11“Here is a data integration platform sold to IT Directors and Finance Managers in European enterprises to eliminate manual re-keying between TMC back-office and accounting systems.”
in 24d — Fri Jun 12“Here is a French or German startup providing a travel-to-ERP data connector that automates hotel invoice coding and booking data synchronization for mid-market European companies.”
in 25d — Sat Jun 13“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
in 26d — Sun Jun 14“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”
in 27d — Mon Jun 15“Here is a European startup adjacent to travel ERP integration providing NDC content aggregation middleware, travel data warehousing, or booking analytics infrastructure for enterprises.”

Reloa0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. AARON GROUP90MATCHThe company offers 'Symphony Corporate Self Booking Tool (B2B)' for corporate customers, and 'Symphony API' supporting NDC/GDS/low-cost airlines. This aligns with a corporate travel booking and distribution platform (Part A), and specifically with TMC workflow automation (Part B, tmc_workflow_platform) as it targets OTAs, TMCs, and Corporates with flight retailing solutions.https://aarongroup.net
Travel Data + Analytics GmbH10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails because the company provides data and analytics on the German travel market, which is not B2B software, SaaS, or tech-enabled services that automate or digitize hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://traveldataanalytics.de
Bratsi90MATCHSTEP A is met as Bratsi delivers SAP Concur implementation services for travel and expense management, which is a B2B tech-enabled service in the corporate travel domain. STEP B is met as the company aligns with the 'travel_erp_integrations' angle by providing SAP Concur implementation, acting as an ERP connector for corporate travel platforms.https://brat-si.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Circula (anchor)
2. Mobilexpense (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 5 companies → evaluated: 5 for sforbt

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.

LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).

↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 172 leads

Lightfield15NO MATCHThe company describes itself as a next-generation CRM that captures and organizes customer interactions, which is a generic productivity tool and does not align with the European corporate travel technology vertical as required by STEP 1.A.https://startups.gallery/companies/lightfield
Synter Media AI10NO MATCHThe company's core business is an AI platform for paid media campaign execution, which is a generic marketing automation tool. It does not align with any specific angle within the European corporate travel technology domain (hotel booking, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments, or travel ERP integrations). Therefore, it fails STEP A of the gate question.https://www.billhartzer.com/ai/synter-ai-platform-
Greenly0NO MATCHThe company's core business is 'Carbon management platform for SMBs', which is outside the European corporate travel technology domain. This fails STEP A.https://startups.gallery/companies/greenly
9fin0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company, 9fin, is described as an 'AI-native platform for global debt markets' which is a financial technology business, not a corporate travel technology domain. It does not automate or digitize hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://www.scalablepod.it/en/startup/9fin/
Upvest10NO MATCHThe company's core business is fintech infrastructure for investment platforms, not corporate travel technology. It fails STEP A of the gate question.https://partechpartners.com/companies/upvest
Multiverse0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company's core business is workforce upskilling in tech, data, and AI, not European corporate travel technology. It provides learning and development solutions, which is a generic HR/L&D offering, not a specialized travel tech solution.https://www.multiverse.io/about/our-mission
Mintlify Editor0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) failed. Mintlify Editor is a documentation platform, not a corporate travel technology solution. It does not provide services for hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://www.mintlify.com/docs/quickstartv7_3
Mixpanel Events 2.00NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) failed. Next.js 160NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails as Next.js 16 is a React framework and SaaS starter kits, which is a developer toolset, not a corporate travel technology solution. It does not automate hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://makerkit.dev/blog/tutorials/nextjs-16v7_3
Slack0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Slack is a team communication and collaboration platform, which is a generic HR/productivity tool and not specifically within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://slack.com/helpv7_3
Amplitude Guides and Surveys10NO MATCHAmplitude's core business is a digital analytics platform providing user engagement and onboarding tools, which is not within the European corporate travel technology domain. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

agentscope-ai20NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1. The description is too generic to determine if it's B2B, what specific workflows it addresses, or its target geography.http://qwenpaw.agentscope.io/
agentscope-ai0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) failed. Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

🟪 90/100 AARON GROUP
